One studio. Three ways of building.

ABOUT ELECTRONIC ARTEFACTS

One studio, four connected ways of building.

Electronic Artefacts is an independent creative technology studio combining client commissions, proprietary software, open-source tools and research-led cultural publishing. Strategy, design and engineering are connected to the questions of knowledge, memory and responsibility that shape a durable digital system.

Client work makes the practice accountable.

Commissions place ideas under real constraints: users, budgets, existing infrastructure, schedules and maintenance. The studio can define a new product, improve a platform or investigate a system that must be recovered.

Proprietary programs retain reusable logic.

VASTE and related programs preserve architecture, methods and production knowledge that should outlive one delivery. They create a technical spine for future platforms, pilots and implementation partnerships.

Open-source tools make methods inspectable.

Voice Capture Studio and related public software expose implementation choices, documentation and boundaries so useful tooling can be tested, cited and reused outside a single commission.

Research and publishing keep the field open.

Publications, experiments, music and archives investigate questions whose value is not limited to an immediate brief. Those investigations feed back into software, interfaces and client decisions.

A connected ecosystem with explicit roles.

Research frames questions, programs retain reusable logic, open software exposes reusable tools, projects apply the work and archives preserve the resulting evidence. This structure keeps experimentation legible while allowing each public work to stand on its own.

See the workExplore researchContact the studio
